item.title: 【Augmenting Your Dev Org with Agentic Teams】通过代理团队增强您的开发组织
item.description:
【

I thought I was fast. The data disagreed.

I’m 43, and I recently took my pit bike to the track, convinced I still had it. The lap times said otherwise. Turns out I’m not the only one with a perception gap.

】

我以为我很快。数据不一致。

我 43 岁了，最近我带着我的场地自行车去赛道，确信我仍然拥有它。单圈时间却另有说法。事实证明我并不是唯一一个存在认知差距的人。

item.title: 【Why Kotlin Multiplatform is a Game-Changer for Startup Teams】为什么 Kotlin 多平台能够改变初创团队的游戏规则
item.description:
【

For early-stage startups, the most valuable resources are time, engineering capacity, and a clear business vision. The key difference between them and larger corporations is that startups typically do not have extensive budgets or specialized experts for each platform. They also do not have long release cycles. Their survival in a competitive business environment depends on speed — the ability to ship and iterate quickly — and on validating product-market fit before competitors do.

There is also a significant technical dilemma startups must consider: whether to build for multiple ecosystems from the outset. A modern IT product is generally expected to support Android, iOS, and desktop platforms. The challenge is that, as noted earlier, startups rarely have dedicated engineers for each platform. Moreover, building products across different ecosystems requires multiple codebases and isolated workflows, which further complicates development. This leads to higher costs, slower delivery, and duplicated effort across the entire product organization.

】

对于早期初创公司来说，最宝贵的资源是时间、工程能力和清晰的业务愿景。它们与大公司之间的主要区别在于，初创公司通常没有针对每个平台的大量预算或专业专家。它们的发布周期也不长。他们在竞争激烈的商业环境中的生存取决于速度（快速交付和迭代的能力）以及在竞争对手之前验证产品与市场的契合度。

初创公司还必须考虑一个重大的技术困境：是否从一开始就构建多个生态系统。现代 IT 产品通常期望支持 Android、iOS 和桌面平台。正如前面提到的，挑战在于初创公司很少为每个平台配备专门的工程师。此外，跨不同生态系统构建产品需要多个代码库和独立的工作流程，这进一步使开发变得复杂。这会导致整个产品组织的成本更高、交付速度更慢以及重复工作。

item.title: 【AI in Patient Portals: From Digital Access to Intelligent Healthcare Experiences】患者门户中的人工智能：从数字访问到智能医疗体验
item.description:
【

Patient portals across mobile, web, and kiosk platforms have become the primary digital touchpoints between healthcare organizations and patients. The inception of these portals began with digitizing paper check-in forms and has evolved into full-fledged mobile and web applications that allow patients to view lab results, schedule appointments, and communicate with providers.

As patient expectations rise — along with advances in consumer technology — traditional rule-based portals are no longer sufficient. This is where Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming patient portals from static systems into intelligent, adaptive healthcare experiences.

】

跨移动、网络和信息亭平台的患者门户已成为医疗保健组织和患者之间的主要数字接触点。这些门户网站的诞生始于纸质登记表的数字化，现已发展成为成熟的移动和网络应用程序，允许患者查看实验室结果、安排预约以及与提供者沟通。

随着患者期望的提高以及消费者技术的进步，传统的基于规则的门户已不再足够。这就是人工智能 (AI) 正在将患者门户从静态系统转变为智能、自适应医疗保健体验的地方。

item.title: 【Beyond Django and Flask: How FastAPI Became Python's Fastest-Growing Framework for Production APIs】超越 Django 和 Flask：FastAPI 如何成为 Python 增长最快的生产 API 框架
item.description:
【

In December 2025, FastAPI achieved what many thought was impossible just three years ago: it surpassed Flask in GitHub stars, reaching 88,000 compared to Flask's 68,400. This isn't just a popularity contest. It represents a fundamental architectural shift in how professional developers are building production APIs in 2026.

The numbers from early 2026 tell an even more compelling story. According to the latest JetBrains Python Developer Survey, FastAPI jumped from 29% to 38% adoption among Python developers in 2025 — a staggering 40% year-over-year increase. The 2025 Stack Overflow survey confirmed the trend with a five-percentage-point surge, making it one of the most significant shifts in the web framework landscape.

】

2025 年 12 月，FastAPI 实现了三年前许多人认为不可能的目标：它在 GitHub 星数上超越了 Flask，达到 88,000，而 Flask 为 68,400。这不仅仅是一场人气竞赛。它代表了专业开发人员在 2026 年构建生产 API 方式的根本性架构转变。

2026 年初的数字讲述了一个更加引人注目的故事。根据最新的 JetBrains Python 开发者调查，到 2025 年，FastAPI 在 Python 开发者中的采用率将从 29% 跃升至 38%，同比增幅高达 40%。 2025 年 Stack Overflow 调查证实了这一趋势，增幅达 5 个百分点，使其成为 Web 框架领域最重大的变化之一。

item.title: 【How to Use AWS IAM Identity Center for Scalable, Compliant Cloud Access Control】如何使用 AWS IAM Identity Center 实现可扩展、合规的云访问控制
item.description:
【

What Is AWS IAM Identity Center?

Think of IAM Identity Center (previously AWS SSO) as the gatekeeper to your cloud environment. Its role is to make sure only the right users or services gain access to your AWS resources, and only with the exact permissions they need. Built as a cloud-based identity management service, it handles authentication and authorization for AWS accounts and other supported business applications, all from a single pane of glass.

The Core Mission

  • Centralized access: Decide who gets in and what they can do from a single control point.
  • Seamless authentication: Users log in once and move across authorized applications.
  • Extensive integrations: Integrates with AWS accounts, enterprise directories, and third-party services.

How Does Identity Center Fit Into AWS?

AWS environments can quickly become complex, spanning multiple accounts, regions, stacks, and workloads. In the past, managing identities, passwords, and permissions across all of them was a headache. Then came the push for single sign-on (SSO), so users wouldn’t have to juggle multiple logins. That’s where AWS IAM Identity Center steps in.

】

什么是 AWS IAM 身份中心？

将 IAM Identity Center（以前称为 AWS SSO）视为您的云环境的看门人。其作用是确保只有正确的用户或服务才能访问您的 AWS 资源，并且只能获得他们所需的确切权限。它作为基于云的身份管理服务而构建，可通过单一管理平台处理 AWS 帐户和其他受支持的业务应用程序的身份验证和授权。

核心使命

  • 集中访问：从单个控制点决定谁可以进入以及他们可以做什么。
  • 无缝身份验证：用户登录一次即可在授权的应用程序之间移动。
  • 广泛集成：与 AWS 账户、企业目录和第三方服务集成。

Identity Center 如何融入 AWS？

AWS 环境很快就会变得复杂，跨越多个账户、区域、堆栈和工作负载。过去，管理所有这些身份、密码和权限是一件令人头疼的事情。随后推出了单点登录 (SSO)，这样用户就不必同时进行多次登录。这就是 AWS IAM Identity Center 发挥作用的地方。

item.title: 【Retries Are a Denial-of-Wallet Attack Waiting to Happen】重试是等待发生的拒绝钱包攻击
item.description:
【

The invoice arrived on a Tuesday. Forty-seven thousand dollars for Lambda invocations across a weekend nobody was working. The team lead stared at CloudWatch metrics — normal traffic Friday afternoon, then a cliff of timeouts starting around 21:00. What followed wasn't an attack. No credential leak, no bot swarm. Just the application eating itself alive through retries, each failed request spawning three more, those spawning nine, the exponential curve steepening until AWS started provisioning containers faster than anyone could hit the "stop" button.

This is what we mean by Denial-of-Wallet. Not malicious. Self-inflicted.

】

发票于周二送达。一个无人工作的周末，Lambda 调用花费了 47,000 美元。团队负责人盯着 CloudWatch 指标——周五下午流量正常，然后从 21:00 左右开始出现超时悬崖。接下来发生的并不是攻击。没有凭证泄漏，没有机器人群。只是应用程序通过重试将自己活活吃掉，每个失败的请求都会再产生三个，最后产生九个，指数曲线变得陡峭，直到 AWS 开始以比任何人按下“停止”按钮的速度更快的速度配置容器。

这就是我们所说的“拒绝钱包”。没有恶意。自己造成的。

item.title: 【The Inner Loop Is Eating The Outer Loop】内循环正在吞噬外循环
item.description:
【

For as long as most of us have been building software, there has been a clean split in the development lifecycle: the inner loop and the outer loop.

The inner loop is where a developer lives day to day. Write code, run it locally, check if it works, iterate. It is fast, tight, and personal. The outer loop is everything after you push. Continuous integration pipelines, integration tests, staging deployments, and code review. It is comprehensive but slow, and for good reason. Running your entire test suite against every keystroke would be insane. So we optimized: fast feedback locally, thorough validation later.

】

自从我们大多数人一直在构建软件以来，开发生命周期就已经明确划分：内循环和外循环。

内循环是开发人员日常工作的地方。编写代码，在本地运行它，检查它是否有效，然后迭代。它快速、紧凑且个性化。外循环就是你push之后的一切。持续集成管道、集成测试、分阶段部署和代码审查。它很全面，但速度很慢，这是有充分理由的。针对每次击键运行整个测试套件将是疯狂的。所以我们优化了：本地快速反馈，后期彻底验证。

item.title: 【Square, SumUp, Shopify: Data Streaming for Real-Time Point-of-Sale (POS)】Square、SumUp、Shopify：实时销售点 (POS) 的数据流
item.description:
【

Point-of-Sale (POS) systems are no longer just cash registers. They are becoming real-time, connected platforms that handle payments, manage inventory, personalize customer experiences, and feed business intelligence. Small and medium-sized merchants can now access capabilities once reserved for enterprise retailers. Mobile payment platforms like Square, SumUp, and Shopify make it easy to sell anywhere and integrate sales channels seamlessly.

At the same time, data streaming technologies such as Apache Kafka and Apache Flink are transforming retail operations. They enable instant insights and automated actions across every store, website, and supply chain partner.

】

销售点 (POS) 系统不再只是收银机。它们正在成为处理支付、管理库存、个性化客户体验和提供商业智能的实时互联平台。中小型商家现在可以使用曾经为企业零售商保留的功能。 Square、SumUp 和 Shopify 等移动支付平台让您可以轻松地在任何地方进行销售并无缝集成销售渠道。

与此同时，Apache Kafka 和 Apache Flink 等数据流技术正在改变零售运营。它们可以为每个商店、网站和供应链合作伙伴提供即时洞察和自动化操作。

item.title: 【Best Practices to Make Your Data AI-Ready】让您的数据为 AI 做好准备的最佳实践
item.description:
【

The key problem organizations encounter when implementing AI is not the technology itself, but the data needed to feed AI models. Many companies have plenty of data, but when it comes to quality, it often turns out to be messy, inconsistent, or biased. If you want your AI investments to deliver real value, you must make your data AI‑ready first.

Below, I share some best practices for building an AI-ready culture and establishing a data management framework that ensures high-quality data pipelines for AI initiatives.

】

组织在实施人工智能时遇到的关键问题不是技术本身，而是为人工智能模型提供所需的数据。许多公司拥有大量数据，但就质量而言，这些数据往往是混乱的、不一致的或有偏见的。如果您希望 AI 投资带来真正的价值，您必须先让您的数据AI 就绪。

下面，我将分享一些构建人工智能就绪文化和建立数据管理框架的最佳实践，以确保人工智能计划的高质量数据管道。

item.title: 【Why Low Latency Financial Systems Still Favor Determinism】为什么低延迟金融系统仍然青睐确定性
item.description:
【

In many areas of software development, latency is treated as a performance metric that can be improved over time. In parts of financial infrastructure, latency is handled differently. It is often a fixed constraint that shapes system design from the outset.

Trading, risk evaluation, and market connectivity systems operate under strict timing requirements. They are expected to behave consistently under load, during peak market activity, and when components fail. Variability under these conditions is treated as risk, not just inefficiency.

】

在软件开发的许多领域，延迟被视为可以随着时间的推移而改进的性能指标。在部分金融基础设施中，延迟的处理方式有所不同。它通常是从一开始就影响系统设计的固定约束。

交易、风险评估和市场连接系统在严格的时间要求下运行。它们预计在负载下、市场活动高峰期间以及组件出现故障时表现一致。这些条件下的变化被视为风险，而不仅仅是低效率。
